feed.title: SAP Community - Settlement Management
feed.subtitle: Settlement Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Best Practice activation fails in 39D (DE) (2023 FP01)
entry.content:
Dear community,

we are activating Best Practice 39D (DE_2R7_OP), this error occurs:

When checking view VVC_T683_MA, there already is an entry with RETO:
SAP message is open, but can someone else help us?
Best Regards,
Daniel Lippmann

entry.title: CCM : delta accrual after partial settlement
entry.content:
Hi Comunity,

I am wondering why standard sap behaves the following way :

1. I create an invoice

2. I create a Condition contract that should have had covered this invoice

3. I create another invoice, same as 1., so that CC created at 2. is dully called (accruals REA1 ok)

4. I do a partial settlement, all ok, invoice 1. and 3. considered, accruals from 3. cancelled.

5. I run a delta : SAP created accruals for invoice 1. IMHO, it should not, as this invoice is already covered by partial settlement.

Any idea what I could be doing wrong ?

- I tried to create a delta before the partial at step 4.: It does contain values for invoice 1., ok. But still same result, accruals recreated after the delta.

- I checked WCOCOF entries, they look ok

- I tried both with an automatic document (WZR1 based on proforma) and manual one (WB2R_DOC_ENTRY), results are the same

 

Thanks for your help !

Regards, Olivier

entry.title: Is it possible to use same Condition contract (0S01) for both AR & AP
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Is it possible to use 0S01 Condition contract,  for both Accounts payable and Accounts receivables without making any config changes? If not what changes we need to make?

Appreciate your inputs in this regard

entry.title: Sales Commissions for external agents & Co.
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

is it possible to use the SD commission from ECC in S/4HANA until it expires?

Kind regards
Hüseyin Günes

entry.title: Production and Maintenance Order Settlement
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question about Production and Maintenance Order Settlement. When I settle Production order with consumes I see also quantities that are settled in accounting document, while in Maintenance order, always with consumes this does not happen.

Is there any setting that I'm missing? Or this is the standard SAP behaviour?

Thank you,

Pietro

entry.title: Settlement management rebates
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I'm trying to create condition contract for rebates using WCOCO transaction code in theSettlement Management but i'm unable to see no contracts types in the drop down. I have checked the config in spro i could not find any issue in the config. I'm attaching the screenshot for reference. Kindly suggest what config to be done so that the condition contracts are visible in the drop down button.

entry.title: Workflow hierarchy approval setup for condition contract
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can anyone share configuration step by step for hierarchy approval process for condition contract?

Example: 1. contract will be approved by employee A, if employee A approved it then it will go to employee B. If employee A reject it then it goes to contract creator, with email notification.

2. contract will be approved by employee B, if employee A approved it then it will go to employee C. If employee B reject it then it goes to contract creator, with email notification.

 

Is there any Fiori app or tcode or report where user can check contract no wise approval details?

entry.title: Net value is 0 in settlement document SD
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I want to mention a problem that I faced. 

I'm trying to create settlement document with WB2R_SC. When I create the document net and gross value does not update. I can see prices and conditions in item details but net and gross value is zero. How can i solve this problem? 

I'm waiting for your solution advises.
Thanks.

entry.title: Condition Contract - Fixed Amount Distribution in Settlement Document
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

In condition contract, percentage base rebate calculation work as per expectation. It is considering negative value such as credit note or cancel invoice value for rebate base value and accrual also updated in negative amount.

But in case of fixed about based rebate calculation, it's considering all value for rebate base value but accruals is always positive. So, I want to know if this is a standard behavior of fixed amount-based rebate calculation or through configuration/customization we can avoid considering negative value.

Thanks,

Arindam

entry.title: CCM Workflow Report
entry.content: Do we have any standard report or app to see the list of pending workflow against each users in CCM.

entry.title: Tcode/Fiori apps for checking approval status
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Is there any tcode or fiori apps where user can check condition contract number wise release status or condition contract number wise approval status?

Thanks,

Arindam Sur

entry.title: Settlement management - incorrect cancellation of accruals
entry.content:
I have Settlement Management set up with settings very close to standard and I have the following issue. 

There are 3 settlement dates: 1 - Delta accrual, 2 - Delta accrual, 3 - Partial settlement (and this goes on as accruals are posted every month and partial settlement every quarter). 
1  posts the accrual OK, 252 USD - correct - both RES1 and RED1 condition populated, but only RES1 active
2 cancels 1, then post, 630 USD - correct - - both RES1 and RED1 condition populated, but only RES1 active. REA2 is there as cancellation with -252 (correct)
3 (partial settlement) cancels both 1 and 2 with REA2 -882 which should only be -630, then posts full quarter amount (correctly). Here, accrual 1 should not be cancelled again, only accrual 2. 
How can this be fixed?

entry.title: remove the deletion indicator from a condition contract which has been logically deleted
entry.content:
Hi,

how can we remove the deletion indicator from a condition contract which has been logically deleted. i have checked multiple transaction but not found any option to do so.

entry.title: Set up of Accrual condition type REA1 in Standard pricing procedure RVAA01
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

As part of settlement management, we need to maintain accrual condition type REA1 in the
sales pricing procedure RVA01. This condition type is also maintained as data records in the
condition contract(WCOCO) with the respective percentage.

Can someone please explain the details to add the accrual condition type REA1 in the sales standard pricing procedure RVAA01 using transaction code V/08.

What to fill in columns from-To, sub-total, any routines and account/accrual keys for accrual condition type REA1?

Thank you.

entry.title: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

we have implemented the Condition Contract for sales in an S/4HANA Project, and introduced the manual delta accrual document to manage both data migration and unplanned delta accruals.

While we are able to post manual delta accruals correctly using the RENT pricing condition (the CC is also updated inserting a new date in the settlement calenar), we encounter errors when issuing follow-on documents:

  1. Without specifying the RENT condition in the Condition Type Group for Accruals (as per standard configuration):

    • Automatic delta accruals are processed correctly.
    • However, the amount booked as "manual delta accrual" is not reversed during the final settlement run.

  2. With RENT condition specified in the Condition Type Group for Accruals (RENT condition type assigned to "Tgt AccReversal" as REA2 and "Tgt CType Daccr" as RED2):

    • The first automatic delta accrual reverses the amount booked as "manual delta accrual."

Could you please provide guidance on how to resolve these issues?

Thank you

Michele

entry.title: Mandatory business volume selection criteria in Condition Contract
entry.content:
Hi all,

We experienced a case where the user created a Condition Contract for an IC customer. In the business Volume selection criteria the user only entered customer number and distribution channel.

This IC customer is used across several sales organizations belonging to different company codes. So, what happened is that accruals was created for 2 company codes for this contract. This in turn caused troubles when we tried to reverse the accruals as we have not allowed the settlement program to post to different company codes (Badi not active). We solved this by simulating that the badi was active in the debugger and then reverse the accruals.

But my question is: Is there any way to make a specific business volume selection criteria mandatory entry? I want to make sales organization ad a mandatory business volume selection criteria.

Br,

Petter

entry.title: Maximum Limit on Rebate | Settlement Management | Condition Contract
entry.content:
Is it possible to achive the below objectives with standard configuration

1.Restrict the settlment amount for a rebate with a maximum value?

Example: Rebate of  5% maintained in the conditions, but the maximum rebate a customer can recieve should be restricted with a value say 1000$ 

2.Rebate should only apply on the quantity after the scale value?

Example: Rebate of 5 % is maintained in the condiitons with a scale of 100EA. When the business volume is 120EA, the rebate of 5 % should apply only on 20EA and not on the 120 EA.

entry.title: Account determination for KORS
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm configuring rebates delta accruals and I need to set different accounts deppending on condition types. However, during delta accrual settlement, I get condition type KORS and I cannot set differente accounts to it.

I'm using different account keys for each account determination, but KORS condition type always takes account key 0S1. 

How can I set different accounts to this condition?

entry.title: CJ88 User Exit for COPA Settlement
entry.content:
Dear Expert, 

I am facing an issue with CJ88 settlement to PSG. 

I have a COPA Account Based and I know that the characteristics reported in COPA Line Items are the ones reported in WBS Settlement rules filled in the WBS Master Data, but I want to add a characteristics (Material Group) based on Product reported in the ACDOCA line item that I am settling.

I have tried to see COPA Enhancements, in order to derive the material group, but they are not useful because they works on characteristics already filled and the Product is not in these one. 

Any suggestion how to works? Maybe a CJ88 User Exit?

Thanks

Armando

entry.title: Sales Condition Contract Manual settlement document shows positive amount while automatic settlement
entry.content:
When I am creating manual settlement document via WB2R_DOC_ENTRY system created a settlement document with positive amount, when I check conditions I found RENT condition type at the item level.

while when I create automatic settlement document through WB2R_SC it shows negative value, please help me understand.

looking forward to understand the relationship.

thanks in advance for your help.

Rafiq
